text.NRSV: In the days when the judges ruled, there was a famine in the land, and a certain man of Bethlehem in Judah went to live in the country of Moab, he and his wife and two sons.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the days when the Judges administered justice, that there was a famine in the land,—and so a certain man went his way, out of Bethlehem-judah to sojourn in the country of Moab, he, and his wife, and his two sons.
text.KJV: Now it came to pass in the days when the judges ruled, that there was a famine in the land. And a certain man of Beth–lehem–judah went to sojourn in the country of Moab, he, and his wife, and his two sons.
text.NABRE: Chapter 1 Naomi in Moab. Once back in the time of the judges there was a famine in the land; so a man from Bethlehem of Judah left home with his wife and two sons to reside on the plateau of Moab.
text.DRB: In the days of one Judge, when the Judges ruled, there came a famine in the Land. And there went a man of Bethlehem Juda to sojourn in the land of Moab, with his wife and two children.

text.NRSV: The name of the man was Elimelech and the name of his wife Naomi, and the names of his two sons were Mahlon and Chilion; they were Ephrathites from Bethlehem in Judah. They went into the country of Moab and remained there.
text.Rotherham: And, the name of the man, was Elimelech, and, the name of his wife, Naomi, and, the name of his two sons, Mahlon and Chilion—Ephrathites out of Bethlehem-judah,—so they came into the country of Moab, and remained there.
text.KJV: And the name of the man was Elimelech, and the name of his wife Naomi, and the name of his two sons Mahlon and Chilion, Ephrathites of Beth–lehem–judah. And they came into the country of Moab, and continued there.
text.NABRE: The man was named Elimelech, his wife Naomi, and his sons Mahlon and Chilion; they were Ephrathites from Bethlehem of Judah. Some time after their arrival on the plateau of Moab,
text.DRB: Himself was called Elimelech, and his wife Noemi: and his two sons, the one Mahalon, and the other Chelion, Ephraites of Bethlehem Juda. And entering into the country of Moab, they abode there.

text.NRSV: But Elimelech, the husband of Naomi, died, and she was left with her two sons.
text.Rotherham: And Elimelech, husband of Naomi, died,—and she was left, she and her two sons.
text.KJV: And Elimelech Naomi’s husband died; and she was left, and her two sons.
text.NABRE: Elimelech, the husband of Naomi, died, and she was left with her two sons.
text.DRB: And Elimelech the husband of Noemi died: and she remained with her sons.

text.NRSV: These took Moabite wives; the name of the one was Orpah and the name of the other Ruth. When they had lived there about ten years,
text.Rotherham: And they took them wives, of the women of Moab, the name of the one, was Orpah, and, the name of the other, Ruth,—and they dwelt there, about ten years.
text.KJV: And they took them wives of the women of Moab; the name of the one was Orpah, and the name of the other Ruth: and they dwelled there about ten years.
text.NABRE: They married Moabite women, one named Orpah, the other Ruth. When they had lived there about ten years,
text.DRB: Who took wives of the Moabites, of the which one was called Orpha, and the other Ruth. And they abode there ten years,

text.NRSV: both Mahlon and Chilion also died, so that the woman was left without her two sons and her husband.
text.Rotherham: And, they also, both, died, Mahlon and Chilion,—so the woman was bereft of her two sons, and of her husband.
text.KJV: And Mahlon and Chilion died also both of them; and the woman was left of her two sons and her husband.
text.NABRE: both Mahlon and Chilion died also, and the woman was left with neither her two boys nor her husband.
text.DRB: and both died, to wit, Mahalon and Chelion: and the woman remained destitute of her two children & her husband.

text.NRSV: Then she started to return with her daughters-in-law from the country of Moab, for she had heard in the country of Moab that the Lord had considered his people and given them food.
text.Rotherham: Then she arose, she and her daughters-in-law, and returned out of the country of Moab,—for she had heard, in the country of Moab, how that Yahweh had visited his people, in giving unto them, bread.
text.KJV: Then she arose with her daughters in law, that she might return from the country of Moab: for she had heard in the country of Moab how that the Lord had visited his people in giving them bread.
text.NABRE: She and her daughters-in-law then prepared to go back from the plateau of Moab because word had reached her there that the Lord had seen to his people’s needs and given them food.
text.DRB: And she arose to go into her country with both her daughters in law from the country of Moab: for she had heard that our Lord had respected his people, & had given them victuals.

text.NRSV: So she set out from the place where she had been living, she and her two daughters-in-law, and they went on their way to go back to the land of Judah.
text.Rotherham: So she went forth out of the dwelling-place where she had remained, and her two daughters-in-law with her,—and they went on the way, to return unto the land of Judah.
text.KJV: Wherefore she went forth out of the place where she was, and her two daughters in law with her; and they went on the way to return unto the land of Judah.
text.NABRE: She and her two daughters-in-law left the place where they had been living. On the road back to the land of Judah,
text.DRB: She therefore went forth from the place of her peregrination, with both her daughters in law: and being now set in the way to return into the Land of Juda,

text.NRSV: But Naomi said to her two daughters-in-law, “Go back each of you to your mother’s house. May the Lord deal kindly with you, as you have dealt with the dead and with me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Naomi to her two daughters-in-law: Go return, each one to the house of her mother,—Yahweh deal with you in lovingkindness, as ye have dealt with the dead, and with me.
text.KJV: And Naomi said unto her two daughters in law, Go, return each to her mother’s house: the Lord deal kindly with you, as ye have dealt with the dead, and with me.
text.NABRE: Naomi said to her daughters-in-law, “Go back, each of you to your mother’s house. May the Lord show you the same kindness as you have shown to the deceased and to me.
text.DRB: she said to them: Go into your mother's house, our Lord do mercy with you, as you have done with the dead and with me.

text.NRSV: The Lord grant that you may find security, each of you in the house of your husband.” Then she kissed them, and they wept aloud.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh grant you, that ye may find a place of rest, each one in the house of her husband,—And she kissed them, and they lifted up their voice, and wept.
text.KJV: The Lord grant you that ye may find rest, each of you in the house of her husband. Then she kissed them; and they lifted up their voice, and wept.
text.NABRE: May the Lord guide each of you to find a husband and a home in which you will be at rest.” She kissed them good-bye, but they wept aloud,
text.DRB: Grant he unto you to find rest in the house of your husbands, which you shall take. And she kissed them. Who lifting up their voice began to weep,

text.NRSV: They said to her, “No, we will return with you to your people.”
text.Rotherham: And they said to her,—With thee, will we return, unto thy people.
text.KJV: And they said unto her, Surely we will return with thee unto thy people.
text.NABRE: crying, “No! We will go back with you, to your people.”
text.DRB: & to say: We will go on with thee to thy people.

text.NRSV: But Naomi said, “Turn back, my daughters, why will you go with me? Do I still have sons in my womb that they may become your husbands?
text.Rotherham: Then said Naomi—Go back, my daughters! wherefore should ye journey with me? Have I, yet, sons in my womb, that they should become your, husbands?
text.KJV: And Naomi said, Turn again, my daughters: why will ye go with me? are there yet any more sons in my womb, that they may be your husbands?
text.NABRE: Naomi replied, “Go back, my daughters. Why come with me? Have I other sons in my womb who could become your husbands?
text.DRB: To whom she answered: Return my daughters, why come you with me? Shall I have sons any more in my womb, that you may hope for husbands of me?

text.NRSV: Turn back, my daughters, go your way, for I am too old to have a husband. Even if I thought there was hope for me, even if I should have a husband tonight and bear sons,
text.Rotherham: Go back, my daughters, go your way, for I am too old to have a husband,—If I should say, I have, hope, if I should, even to-night have a husband, and should, even bear sons,
text.KJV: Turn again, my daughters, go your way; for I am too old to have an husband. If I should say, I have hope, if I should have an husband also to night, and should also bear sons;
text.NABRE: Go, my daughters, for I am too old to marry again. Even if I had any such hope, or if tonight I had a husband and were to bear sons,
text.DRB: Return my daughters, and go your ways: for I am now spent with old age, and not fit for wedlock. Although I might conceive this night, and bear children,

text.NRSV: would you then wait until they were grown? Would you then refrain from marrying? No, my daughters, it has been far more bitter for me than for you, because the hand of the Lord has turned against me.”
text.Rotherham: would ye, therefore, tarry until they were grown? would ye, therefore, shut yourselves up from having husbands? Nay! my daughters, for it is far more bitter for me than for you, that forth hath gone against me, the hand of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Would ye tarry for them till they were grown? would ye stay for them from having husbands? nay, my daughters; for it grieveth me much for your sakes that the hand of the Lord is gone out against me.
text.NABRE: would you wait for them and deprive yourselves of husbands until those sons grew up? No, my daughters, my lot is too bitter for you, because the Lord has extended his hand against me.”
text.DRB: if you would expect till they grow, and be of man's age, you shall be old women before you marry. Do not so my daughters, I beseech you: for your distress doth the more grieve me, & the hand of our Lord is come forth against me.

text.NRSV: Then they wept aloud again. Orpah kissed her mother-in-law, but Ruth clung to her.
text.Rotherham: And they lifted up their voice and wept, yet more. Then Orpah kissed her mother-in- law, but, Ruth, clave unto her.
text.KJV: And they lifted up their voice, and wept again: and Orpah kissed her mother in law; but Ruth clave unto her.
text.NABRE: Again they wept aloud; then Orpah kissed her mother-in-law good-bye, but Ruth clung to her.
text.DRB: Therefore lifting up their voice they began to weep again: Orpha kissed her mother in law, and returned: Ruth cleaved to her mother in law,

text.NRSV: So she said, “See, your sister-in-law has gone back to her people and to her gods; return after your sister-in-law.”
text.Rotherham: And she said—Lo! thy sister-in-law hath gone back, unto her people, and unto her gods,—go thou back, after thy sister-in-law.
text.KJV: And she said, Behold, thy sister in law is gone back unto her people, and unto her gods: return thou after thy sister in law.
text.NABRE: “See now,” she said, “your sister-in-law has gone back to her people and her god. Go back after your sister-in-law!”
text.DRB: to whom Noemi said: Behold thy kinswoman is returned to her people, and to her gods, go with her.

text.NRSV: But Ruth said, “Do not press me to leave you or to turn back from following you! Where you go, I will go; where you lodge, I will lodge; your people shall be my people, and your God my God.
text.Rotherham: And Ruth said—Do not urge me to leave thee, to go back from following thee,—for, whither thou goest, I will go, and, where thou lodgest, I will lodge, thy people, shall be my people, and, thy God, my God;
text.KJV: And Ruth said, Intreat me not to leave thee, or to return from following after thee: for whither thou goest, I will go; and where thou lodgest, I will lodge: thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God:
text.NABRE: But Ruth said, “Do not press me to go back and abandon you! Wherever you go I will go, wherever you lodge I will lodge. Your people shall be my people and your God, my God.
text.DRB: Who answered: Be not against me, to the end that I should leave thee and depart: for whither soever thou shalt go, I will go: and where thou shalt abide, I also will abide. Thy people my people, and thy God my God.

text.NRSV: Where you die, I will die— there will I be buried. May the Lord do thus and so to me, and more as well, if even death parts me from you!”
text.Rotherham: where thou diest, I will die, and, there, will I be buried: So, let Yahweh do to me, and, so, let him add, if, death itself, part me and thee.
text.KJV: Where thou diest, will I die, and there will I be buried: the Lord do so to me, and more also, if ought but death part thee and me.
text.NABRE: Where you die I will die, and there be buried. May the Lord do thus to me, and more, if even death separates me from you!”
text.DRB: The land that shall receive thee dying, in the same will I die: and there will I take a place for my burial. These things do God to me, & these things add he, if death only shall not separate me and thee.

text.NRSV: When Naomi saw that she was determined to go with her, she said no more to her.
text.Rotherham: And, when she saw that, bravely determined, was she to go with her, she ceased entreating her.
text.KJV: When she saw that she was stedfastly minded to go with her, then she left speaking unto her.
text.NABRE: Naomi then ceased to urge her, for she saw she was determined to go with her.
text.DRB: Noemi therefore seeing that Ruth with a steadfast mind had determined to go forward with her, would not be against it, nor persuade her any more to return to her friends:

text.NRSV: So the two of them went on until they came to Bethlehem. When they came to Bethlehem, the whole town was stirred because of them; and the women said, “Is this Naomi?”
text.Rotherham: So, they two, went their way, until they entered Bethlehem. And it came to pass, when they entered Bethlehem, that all the city was moved concerning them, and the women said—Is this Naomi?
text.KJV: So they two went until they came to Beth–lehem. And it came to pass, when they were come to Beth–lehem, that all the city was moved about them, and they said, Is this Naomi?
text.NABRE: The Return to Bethlehem. So they went on together until they reached Bethlehem. On their arrival there, the whole town was excited about them, and the women asked: “Can this be Naomi?”
text.DRB: and they went forth together, and came into Bethlehem. Who being entered into the city, a brute was quickly spread among them: and the women said: This is that Noemi.

text.NRSV: She said to them, “Call me no longer Naomi, call me Mara, for the Almighty has dealt bitterly with me.
text.Rotherham: And she said unto them, Do not call me Naomi [="Sweet"],—call me Mara [="Bitter"], for the Almighty hath dealt very bitterly with me:
text.KJV: And she said unto them, Call me not Naomi, call me Mara: for the Almighty hath dealt very bitterly with me.
text.NABRE: But she said to them, “Do not call me Naomi [‘Sweet’]. Call me Mara [‘Bitter’], for the Almighty has made my life very bitter.
text.DRB: To whom she said: Call me not Noemi (that is to say, beautiful) but call me Mara (that is to say, bitter) because with bitterness hath the Almighty very much replenished me.

text.NRSV: I went away full, but the Lord has brought me back empty; why call me Naomi when the Lord has dealt harshly with me, and the Almighty has brought calamity upon me?”
text.Rotherham: I was full when I departed, but, empty, am I brought back of Yahweh,—wherefore should ye call me Naomi, when, Yahweh, hath given answer against me, and, the Almighty, hath crushed me?
text.KJV: I went out full, and the Lord hath brought me home again empty: why then call ye me Naomi, seeing the Lord hath testified against me, and the Almighty hath afflicted me?
text.NABRE: I went away full, but the Lord has brought me back empty. Why should you call me ‘Sweet,’ since the Lord has brought me to trial, and the Almighty has pronounced evil sentence on me.”
text.DRB: I went forth full, and our Lord hath brought me back empty. Why therefore do you call me Noemi whom our Lord hath humbled, and the Almighty hath afflicted?

text.NRSV: So Naomi returned together with Ruth the Moabite, her daughter-in-law, who came back with her from the country of Moab. They came to Bethlehem at the beginning of the barley harvest.
text.Rotherham: So Naomi returned, and Ruth the Moabitess, her daughter-in-law, with her, who returned out of the country of Moab,—and, they, entered Bethlehem in the beginning of barley harvest.
text.KJV: So Naomi returned, and Ruth the Moabitess, her daughter in law, with her, which returned out of the country of Moab: and they came to Beth–lehem in the beginning of barley harvest.
text.NABRE: Thus it was that Naomi came back with her Moabite daughter-in-law Ruth, who accompanied her back from the plateau of Moab. They arrived in Bethlehem at the beginning of the barley harvest.
text.DRB: Noemi therefore came with Ruth the Moabite her daughter in law, from the Land of her peregrination: and returned into Bethlehem, when barley was first reaped.

text.NRSV: Now Naomi had a kinsman on her husband’s side, a prominent rich man, of the family of Elimelech, whose name was Boaz.
text.Rotherham: Now, Naomi, had an acquaintance of her husband’s, a man of great integrity, of the family of Elimelech,—whose name, was Boaz.
text.KJV: And Naomi had a kinsman of her husband’s, a mighty man of wealth, of the family of Elimelech; and his name was Boaz.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 The Meeting. Naomi had a powerful relative named Boaz, through the clan of her husband Elimelech.
text.DRB: And Elimelech her husband had a cousin, a mighty man and of great riches, named Booz.

text.NRSV: And Ruth the Moabite said to Naomi, “Let me go to the field and glean among the ears of grain, behind someone in whose sight I may find favor.” She said to her, “Go, my daughter.”
text.Rotherham: And Ruth the Moabitess said unto Naomi—Let me go, I pray thee, to the field, and glean ears of corn after him in whose eyes I may find favour. And she said to her—Go, my daughter.
text.KJV: And Ruth the Moabitess said unto Naomi, Let me now go to the field, and glean ears of corn after him in whose sight I shall find grace. And she said unto her, Go, my daughter.
text.NABRE: Ruth the Moabite said to Naomi, “I would like to go and glean grain in the field of anyone who will allow me.” Naomi said to her, “Go ahead, my daughter.”
text.DRB: And Ruth the Moabite said to her mother in law: If thou command, I will go into the field, and gather the ears of corn, that shall escape the hands of the reapers, where soever I shall find the grace of the father of the house favourable towards me. To whom she answered: Go my daughter.

text.NRSV: So she went. She came and gleaned in the field behind the reapers. As it happened, she came to the part of the field belonging to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.
text.Rotherham: So she went her way, and came, and gleaned in the field, after the reapers,—and it happened to her, to light upon the portion of field-land belonging to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.
text.KJV: And she went, and came, and gleaned in the field after the reapers: and her hap was to light on a part of the field belonging unto Boaz, who was of the kindred of Elimelech.
text.NABRE: So she went. The field she entered to glean after the harvesters happened to be the section belonging to Boaz, of the clan of Elimelech.
text.DRB: She went therefore & gathered the ears of corn after the backs of the reapers. And it chanced that the owner of the same field was Booz, who was of the kindred of Elimelech.

text.NRSV: Just then Boaz came from Bethlehem. He said to the reapers, “The Lord be with you.” They answered, “The Lord bless you.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! Boaz coming from Bethlehem, and he said to the reapers, Yahweh, be with you! and they said to him, Yahweh bless thee!
text.KJV: And, behold, Boaz came from Beth–lehem, and said unto the reapers, The Lord be with you. And they answered him, The Lord bless thee.
text.NABRE: Soon, along came Boaz from Bethlehem and said to the harvesters, “The Lord be with you,” and they replied, “The Lord bless you.”
text.DRB: And behold, he came out of Bethlehem, and said to the reapers: Our Lord be with you. Who answered him: Our Lord bless thee.

text.NRSV: Then Boaz said to his servant who was in charge of the reapers, “To whom does this young woman belong?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Boaz to his young man, that was set over the reapers,—Whose is this maiden?
text.KJV: Then said Boaz unto his servant that was set over the reapers, Whose damsel is this?
text.NABRE: Boaz asked the young man overseeing his harvesters, “Whose young woman is this?”
text.DRB: And Booz said to the young man that was overseer of the reapers: Whose maid is this?

text.NRSV: The servant who was in charge of the reapers answered, “She is the Moabite who came back with Naomi from the country of Moab.
text.Rotherham: And the young man that was set over the reapers answered and said,—The Moabitish maiden, is she who came back with Naomi, out of the country of Moab;
text.KJV: And the servant that was set over the reapers answered and said, It is the Moabitish damsel that came back with Naomi out of the country of Moab:
text.NABRE: The young man overseeing the harvesters answered, “She is the young Moabite who came back with Naomi from the plateau of Moab.
text.DRB: To whom he answered: This is that Moabite, which came with Noemi from the country of Moab,

text.NRSV: She said, ‘Please, let me glean and gather among the sheaves behind the reapers.’ So she came, and she has been on her feet from early this morning until now, without resting even for a moment.”
text.Rotherham: and she said—Let me glean, I pray thee, and gather among the sheaves, after the reapers; so she came in, and hath continued from that time, all the morning until just now, and hath not rested in the house, for a little.
text.KJV: And she said, I pray you, let me glean and gather after the reapers among the sheaves: so she came, and hath continued even from the morning until now, that she tarried a little in the house.
text.NABRE: She said, ‘I would like to gather the gleanings into sheaves after the harvesters.’ Ever since she came this morning she has remained here until now, with scarcely a moment’s rest.”
text.DRB: and she desired that she might gather the ears of corn that remain, following the steps of the reapers: and from morning until now she stayeth in the field, and not so much as for a very moment hath she returned home.

text.NRSV: Then Boaz said to Ruth, “Now listen, my daughter, do not go to glean in another field or leave this one, but keep close to my young women.
text.Rotherham: And Boaz said unto Ruth—Hearest thou not, my daughter? Do not go to glean in any other field, neither indeed shalt thou pass on, from hence,—but, here, shalt thou keep fast by my maidens:
text.KJV: Then said Boaz unto Ruth, Hearest thou not, my daughter? Go not to glean in another field, neither go from hence, but abide here fast by my maidens:
text.NABRE: Boaz then spoke to Ruth, “Listen, my daughter. Do not go to glean in anyone else’s field; you are not to leave here. Stay here with my young women.
text.DRB: And Booz said to Ruth: Hear me daughter, go not into another field to gather, neither depart thou from his place: but join thyself to my maids,

text.NRSV: Keep your eyes on the field that is being reaped, and follow behind them. I have ordered the young men not to bother you. If you get thirsty, go to the vessels and drink from what the young men have drawn.”
text.Rotherham: thine eyes, be on the field which they shall reap, and go thou after them, Have I not commanded the young men, that they touch thee not? And, when thou art athirst, then go unto the vessels, and drink of what the young men shall draw.
text.KJV: Let thine eyes be on the field that they do reap, and go thou after them: have I not charged the young men that they shall not touch thee? and when thou art athirst, go unto the vessels, and drink of that which the young men have drawn.
text.NABRE: Watch to see which field is to be harvested, and follow them. Have I not commanded the young men to do you no harm? When you are thirsty, go and drink from the vessels the young people have filled.”
text.DRB: & where they have reaped, follow. For I have commanded my servants, that no man molest thee: but if thou shalt thirst also, go to the fardels, and drink the waters, whereof the servants also do drink.

text.NRSV: Then she fell prostrate, with her face to the ground, and said to him, “Why have I found favor in your sight, that you should take notice of me, when I am a foreigner?”
text.Rotherham: Then she fell upon her face, and bowed herself to the ground,—and said unto him—Wherefore have I found favour in thine eyes, that thou shouldest take notice of me, seeing that, I, am, a stranger?
text.KJV: Then she fell on her face, and bowed herself to the ground, and said unto him, Why have I found grace in thine eyes, that thou shouldest take knowledge of me, seeing I am a stranger?
text.NABRE: Casting herself prostrate upon the ground, she said to him, “Why should I, a foreigner, be favored with your attention?”
text.DRB: Who falling on her face and adoring upon the ground, said to him: Whence cometh this to me, that I should find grace before thine eyes, and that thou wouldst vouchsafe to know me a strange woman?

text.NRSV: But Boaz answered her, “All that you have done for your mother-in-law since the death of your husband has been fully told me, and how you left your father and mother and your native land and came to a people that you did not know before.
text.Rotherham: And Boaz answered, and said to her, It hath been, told, me—all that thou hast done unto thy mother-in-law, since the death of thy husband,—and how thou hast left thy father and thy mother, and the land of thy nativity, and come unto a people whom thou knewest not, aforetime.
text.KJV: And Boaz answered and said unto her, It hath fully been shewed me, all that thou hast done unto thy mother in law since the death of thine husband: and how thou hast left thy father and thy mother, and the land of thy nativity, and art come unto a people which thou knewest not heretofore.
text.NABRE: Boaz answered her: “I have had a complete account of what you have done for your mother-in-law after your husband’s death; you have left your father and your mother and the land of your birth, and have come to a people whom previously you did not know.
text.DRB: To whom he answered: All things have been told me, which thou hast done to thy mother in law after the death of thy husband: and that thou left thy parents, and the land wherein thou wast born, and art come to a people, which before thou knewest not.

text.NRSV: May the Lord reward you for your deeds, and may you have a full reward from the Lord, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come for refuge!”
text.Rotherham: Yahweh recompense thy deed,—and let thy reward be full from Yahweh, the God of Israel, unto whom thou hast come to take refuge under his wings.
text.KJV: The Lord recompense thy work, and a full reward be given thee of the Lord God of Israel, under whose wings thou art come to trust.
text.NABRE: May the Lord reward what you have done! May you receive a full reward from the Lord, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come for refuge.”
text.DRB: Our Lord render unto thee for thy work, and God grant thou mayest receive a full reward of our Lord thy God of Israel, to whom thou art come, & under whose wings thou art fled.

text.NRSV: Then she said, “May I continue to find favor in your sight, my lord, for you have comforted me and spoken kindly to your servant, even though I am not one of your servants.”
text.Rotherham: And she said—Let me find favour in thine eyes, my lord, for that thou hast comforted me, and for that thou hast spoken unto the heart of thy handmaid,—though, I, be not as, one of thine own handmaidens.
text.KJV: Then she said, Let me find favour in thy sight, my lord; for that thou hast comforted me, and for that thou hast spoken friendly unto thine handmaid, though I be not like unto one of thine handmaidens.
text.NABRE: She said, “May I prove worthy of your favor, my lord. You have comforted me. You have spoken to the heart of your servant —and I am not even one of your servants!”
text.DRB: Who said: I have found grace in thine eyes my Lord, which hast comforted me, and hast spoken to the heart of thy handmaid, which am not like to one of thy maids.

text.NRSV: At mealtime Boaz said to her, “Come here, and eat some of this bread, and dip your morsel in the sour wine.” So she sat beside the reapers, and he heaped up for her some parched grain. She ate until she was satisfied, and she had some left over.
text.Rotherham: And Boaz said to her at mealtime—Draw nigh hither, and eat of the bread, and dip thy morsel in the vinegar. So she sat beside the reapers, and there was reached to her parched corn, and she did eat and was satisfied, and left thereof remaining.
text.KJV: And Boaz said unto her, At mealtime come thou hither, and eat of the bread, and dip thy morsel in the vinegar. And she sat beside the reapers: and he reached her parched corn, and she did eat, and was sufficed, and left.
text.NABRE: At mealtime Boaz said to her, “Come here and have something to eat; dip your bread in the sauce.” Then as she sat near the harvesters, he handed her some roasted grain and she ate her fill and had some left over.
text.DRB: And Booz said to her: When the hour shall come to eat, come hither, and eat bread, and dip thy morsel in the vinegar. She therefore sat at the side of the reapers, and she heaped to herself polent, and did eat and was filled, & took the leavings.

text.NRSV: When she got up to glean, Boaz instructed his young men, “Let her glean even among the standing sheaves, and do not reproach her.
text.Rotherham: And, when she rose up to glean, Boaz commanded his young men, saying—Even between the sheaves, let her glean, and reproach her not;
text.KJV: And when she was risen up to glean, Boaz commanded his young men, saying, Let her glean even among the sheaves, and reproach her not:
text.NABRE: As she rose to glean, Boaz instructed his young people: “Let her glean among the sheaves themselves without scolding her,
text.DRB: And from thence she arose to glean the ears of corn after her manner. And Booz commanded his servants, saying: Yea & if she will reap with you, forbid her not:

text.NRSV: You must also pull out some handfuls for her from the bundles, and leave them for her to glean, and do not rebuke her.”
text.Rotherham: yea moreover, draw out, for her, from the bundles,—and leave behind that she may glean it, and rebuke her not.
text.KJV: And let fall also some of the handfuls of purpose for her, and leave them, that she may glean them, and rebuke her not.
text.NABRE: and even drop some handfuls and leave them for her to glean; do not rebuke her.”
text.DRB: and of your own handfuls also cast forth of purpose, and let them remain, that she may gather them without bashfulness, and gathering let no man control her.

text.NRSV: So she gleaned in the field until evening. Then she beat out what she had gleaned, and it was about an ephah of barley.
text.Rotherham: So she gleaned in the field, until the evening,—and beat out that which she had gleaned, and there was about an ephah of barley.
text.KJV: So she gleaned in the field until even, and beat out that she had gleaned: and it was about an ephah of barley.
text.NABRE: She gleaned in the field until evening, and when she beat out what she had gleaned it came to about an ephah of barley,
text.DRB: She gleaned therefore in the field until evening: and that which she had gathered beating with a rod and threshing she found of barley as it were the measure of an ephi, that is, three bushels.

text.NRSV: She picked it up and came into the town, and her mother-in-law saw how much she had gleaned. Then she took out and gave her what was left over after she herself had been satisfied.
text.Rotherham: And she took it up, and came into the city, and, when her mother-in-law had seen what she had gleaned, she brought forth and gave her what had been left remaining, after that she was satisfied.
text.KJV: And she took it up, and went into the city: and her mother in law saw what she had gleaned: and she brought forth, and gave to her that she had reserved after she was sufficed.
text.NABRE: which she took into the town and showed to her mother-in-law. Next she brought out what she had left over from the meal and gave it to her.
text.DRB: Which carrying she returned into the city, and shewed to her mother in law: moreover she brought forth, & gave her of the remains of her meat, wherewith she had been filled.

text.NRSV: Her mother-in-law said to her, “Where did you glean today? And where have you worked? Blessed be the man who took notice of you.” So she told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked, and said, “The name of the man with whom I worked today is Boaz.”
text.Rotherham: And her mother-in-law said to her—In what place hast thou gleaned, today? and where hast thou wrought? May he that took notice of thee, be blessed! So she told her mother-in-law, with whom she had wrought, and said—The name of the man with whom I wrought today, is Boaz.
text.KJV: And her mother in law said unto her, Where hast thou gleaned to day? and where wroughtest thou? blessed be he that did take knowledge of thee. And she shewed her mother in law with whom she had wrought, and said, The man’s name with whom I wrought to day is Boaz.
text.NABRE: So her mother-in-law said to her, “Where did you glean today? Where did you go to work? May the one who took notice of you be blessed!” Then she told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked. “The man at whose place I worked today is named Boaz,” she said.
text.DRB: And her mother in law said to her: Where hast thou gathered today, & where hast thou wrought? Blessed be he that hath had mercy on thee. And she told her with whom she had wrought: and she told the man's name, that he was called Booz.

text.NRSV: Then Naomi said to her daughter-in-law, “Blessed be he by the Lord, whose kindness has not forsaken the living or the dead!” Naomi also said to her, “The man is a relative of ours, one of our nearest kin.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Naomi, to her daughter-in-law—Blessed, be he of Yahweh, who hath not left off his lovingkindness to the living, and to the dead. And Naomi said to her—Near to us, is the man, of our own kinsmen, is he!
text.KJV: And Naomi said unto her daughter in law, Blessed be he of the Lord, who hath not left off his kindness to the living and to the dead. And Naomi said unto her, The man is near of kin unto us, one of our next kinsmen.
text.NABRE: “May he be blessed by the Lord, who never fails to show kindness to the living and to the dead,” Naomi exclaimed to her daughter-in-law. She continued, “This man is a near relative of ours, one of our redeemers.”
text.DRB: To whom Noemi answered: Be he blessed of our Lord: because the same grace, which he had shewed to the living, he hath kept also to the dead. And again she said: The man is our nigh cousin.

text.NRSV: Then Ruth the Moabite said, “He even said to me, ‘Stay close by my young men until they have finished all my harvest.’”
text.Rotherham: And Ruth the Moabitess said,—Yea for he said unto me—By my young men, shalt thou keep fast, until they have ended all my harvest.
text.KJV: And Ruth the Moabitess said, He said unto me also, Thou shalt keep fast by my young men, until they have ended all my harvest.
text.NABRE: “He even told me,” added Ruth the Moabite, “Stay with my young people until they complete my entire harvest.”
text.DRB: And Ruth: This also, quoth she, he commanded me, that so long I should join myself to the reapers, till all the corn were reaped.

text.NRSV: Naomi said to Ruth, her daughter-in-law, “It is better, my daughter, that you go out with his young women, otherwise you might be bothered in another field.”
text.Rotherham: And Naomi said unto Ruth her daughter-in-law,—Good, is it, my daughter, that thou go forth with his maidens, and that they meet thee not, in any other field.
text.KJV: And Naomi said unto Ruth her daughter in law, It is good, my daughter, that thou go out with his maidens, that they meet thee not in any other field.
text.NABRE: “You would do well, my daughter,” Naomi rejoined, “to work with his young women; in someone else’s field you might be insulted.”
text.DRB: To whom her mother in law said: It is better my daughter, that thou go forth with his maids to reap, lest in another man's field some may resist thee.

text.NRSV: So she stayed close to the young women of Boaz, gleaning until the end of the barley and wheat harvests; and she lived with her mother-in-law.
text.Rotherham: So she kept fast by the maidens of Boaz, to glean, until the end of the barley harvest, and the wheat harvest,—and dwelt with her mother-in-law.
text.KJV: So she kept fast by the maidens of Boaz to glean unto the end of barley harvest and of wheat harvest; and dwelt with her mother in law.
text.NABRE: So she stayed gleaning with Boaz’s young women until the end of the barley and wheat harvests.
text.DRB: She therefore joined herself to the maids of Booz: and so long reaped with them, till the barley and the wheat were laid up in the barns.

text.NRSV: Naomi her mother-in-law said to her, “My daughter, I need to seek some security for you, so that it may be well with you.
text.Rotherham: Then Naomi her mother-in-law said to her,—My daughter! shall I not seek for thee a place of rest, in which it may be well with thee?
text.KJV: Then Naomi her mother in law said unto her, My daughter, shall I not seek rest for thee, that it may be well with thee?
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Ruth Again Presents Herself. When Ruth was back with her mother-in-law, Naomi said to her, “My daughter, should I not be seeking a pleasing home for you?
text.DRB: But after that she was returned to her mother in law, she heard of her: My daughter, I will seek thee rest, and will provide that it may be well with thee.

text.NRSV: Now here is our kinsman Boaz, with whose young women you have been working. See, he is winnowing barley tonight at the threshing floor.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, is not, Boaz, of our kindred, with whose maidens thou hast been? Lo! he is winnowing the barley threshing-floor, to-night!
text.KJV: And now is not Boaz of our kindred, with whose maidens thou wast? Behold, he winnoweth barley to night in the threshingfloor.
text.NABRE: Now! Is not Boaz, whose young women you were working with, a relative of ours? This very night he will be winnowing barley at the threshing floor.
text.DRB: This Booz, to whose maids thou art joined in the field, is our nigh kinsman, and this night he winnoweth the barn floor of the barley.

text.NRSV: Now wash and anoint yourself, and put on your best clothes and go down to the threshing floor; but do not make yourself known to the man until he has finished eating and drinking.
text.Rotherham: Thou wilt, therefore, bathe thee, and anoint thee, and put thine apparel upon thee, and go down to the threshing-floor,—do not make thyself known to the man, until he have done eating and drinking.
text.KJV: Wash thyself therefore, and anoint thee, and put thy raiment upon thee, and get thee down to the floor: but make not thyself known unto the man, until he shall have done eating and drinking.
text.NABRE: Now, go bathe and anoint yourself; then put on your best attire and go down to the threshing floor. Do not make yourself known to the man before he has finished eating and drinking.
text.DRB: Wash therefore and anoint thyself, and put on thy better garments, and go down into the barn floor, let no man see thee, till he shall have ended eating & drinking.

text.NRSV: When he lies down, observe the place where he lies; then, go and uncover his feet and lie down; and he will tell you what to do.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, when he lieth down, that thou shalt mark the place where he doth lie, and shalt go in and turn aside the covering of his feet, and lay thee down,—and, he, will tell thee what thou shalt do.
text.KJV: And it shall be, when he lieth down, that thou shalt mark the place where he shall lie, and thou shalt go in, and uncover his feet, and lay thee down; and he will tell thee what thou shalt do.
text.NABRE: But when he lies down, take note of the place where he lies; then go uncover a place at his feet and you lie down. He will then tell you what to do.”
text.DRB: And when he shall go to sleep, mark the place wherein he sleepeth: and thou shalt come, and discover the mantle wherewith he is covered toward his feet, and shall cast thyself down and lie there: and he will tell thee what thou must do.

text.NRSV: She said to her, “All that you tell me I will do.”
text.Rotherham: And she said unto her,—All that thou sayest, will I do.
text.KJV: And she said unto her, All that thou sayest unto me I will do.
text.NABRE: “I will do whatever you say,” Ruth replied.
text.DRB: Who answered: Whatsoever thou shalt command, that will I do.

text.NRSV: So she went down to the threshing floor and did just as her mother-in-law had instructed her.
text.Rotherham: So she went down to the threshing-floor,—and did according to all that her mother-in-law had commanded her.
text.KJV: And she went down unto the floor, and did according to all that her mother in law bade her.
text.NABRE: She went down to the threshing floor and did just as her mother-in-law had instructed her.
text.DRB: And she went down into the barn floor, and did all the things which her mother in law had commanded her.

text.NRSV: When Boaz had eaten and drunk, and he was in a contented mood, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of grain. Then she came stealthily and uncovered his feet, and lay down.
text.Rotherham: And, when Boaz had eaten, and drunk, and his heart was glad, he went in to lie down at the end of the heap of corn. Then came she in softly, and turned aside the covering of his feet, and laid her down.
text.KJV: And when Boaz had eaten and drunk, and his heart was merry, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of corn: and she came softly, and uncovered his feet, and laid her down.
text.NABRE: Boaz ate and drank to his heart’s content, and went to lie down at the edge of the pile of grain. She crept up, uncovered a place at his feet, and lay down.
text.DRB: And when Booz had eaten, and drunken, and was made pleasant, and was gone to sleep by the heap of sheaves, she came closely, and discovering the mantle at his feet, laid herself down.

text.NRSV: At midnight the man was startled, and turned over, and there, lying at his feet, was a woman!
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the middle of the night, that the man started up, and turned,—and lo! a woman, lying at his feet.
text.KJV: And it came to pass at midnight, that the man was afraid, and turned himself: and, behold, a woman lay at his feet.
text.NABRE: Midway through the night, the man gave a start and groped about, only to find a woman lying at his feet.
text.DRB: And behold, when it was now midnight the man was afraid, and troubled: and he saw a woman lying at his feet,

text.NRSV: He said, “Who are you?” And she answered, “I am Ruth, your servant; spread your cloak over your servant, for you are next-of-kin.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Who art, thou? And she said, I, am Ruth, thy handmaid, spread, therefore, thy wing over thy handmaid, for, a kinsman, thou art.
text.KJV: And he said, Who art thou? And she answered, I am Ruth thine handmaid: spread therefore thy skirt over thine handmaid; for thou art a near kinsman.
text.NABRE: “Who are you?” he asked. She replied, “I am your servant Ruth. Spread the wing of your cloak over your servant, for you are a redeemer.”
text.DRB: and said to her: Who art thou? And she answered: I am Ruth thy handmaid: spread thy mantle upon thy servant, because thou art nigh of kin.

text.NRSV: He said, “May you be blessed by the Lord, my daughter; this last instance of your loyalty is better than the first; you have not gone after young men, whether poor or rich.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Blessed, be thou of Yahweh, my daughter, for thou hast made thy last lovingkindness better than the first,—in not following after young men, whether poor, or rich.
text.KJV: And he said, Blessed be thou of the Lord, my daughter: for thou hast shewed more kindness in the latter end than at the beginning, inasmuch as thou followedst not young men, whether poor or rich.
text.NABRE: He said, “May the Lord bless you, my daughter! You have been even more loyal now than before in not going after the young men, whether poor or rich.
text.DRB: And he said: Blessed art thou of our Lord my daughter, and the former mercy thou hast passed with the latter: because thou hast not followed young men either poor or rich.

text.NRSV: And now, my daughter, do not be afraid, I will do for you all that you ask, for all the assembly of my people know that you are a worthy woman.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, my daughter, do not fear, whatsoever thou shalt say, I will do for thee,—for all the gate of my people doth know, that, a virtuous woman, thou art.
text.KJV: And now, my daughter, fear not; I will do to thee all that thou requirest: for all the city of my people doth know that thou art a virtuous woman.
text.NABRE: Now rest assured, my daughter, I will do for you whatever you say; all my townspeople know you to be a worthy woman.
text.DRB: Fear not therefore, but whatsoever thou shalt say to me, I will do to thee. For all the people that dwelleth within the gates of my city know, that thou art a woman of virtue.

text.NRSV: But now, though it is true that I am a near kinsman, there is another kinsman more closely related than I.
text.Rotherham: And, now, although it is true that, a kinsman, am I, yet is there a kinsman nearer than I.
text.KJV: And now it is true that I am thy near kinsman: howbeit there is a kinsman nearer than I.
text.NABRE: Now, I am in fact a redeemer, but there is another redeemer closer than I.
text.DRB: Neither do I deny myself nigh of kin, but there is another nearer than I.

text.NRSV: Remain this night, and in the morning, if he will act as next-of-kin for you, good; let him do it. If he is not willing to act as next-of-kin for you, then, as the Lord lives, I will act as next-of-kin for you. Lie down until the morning.”
text.Rotherham: Tarry the night, and it shall be, in the morning, if he will act as kinsman to thee, well, let him so act, but, if he inclineth not to act as kinsman to thee, then will, I, so act to thee—by the life of Yahweh,—Lie still, until the morning.
text.KJV: Tarry this night, and it shall be in the morning, that if he will perform unto thee the part of a kinsman, well; let him do the kinsman’s part: but if he will not do the part of a kinsman to thee, then will I do the part of a kinsman to thee, as the Lord liveth: lie down until the morning.
text.NABRE: Stay where you are for tonight, and tomorrow, if he will act as redeemer for you, good. But if he will not, as the Lord lives, I will do it myself. Lie there until morning.”
text.DRB: Rest this night: and when morning is come, if he will retain thee by the right of nigh of kindred, the thing is well done, but if he will not, I will take thee without all doubt, our Lord liveth, sleep until morning.

text.NRSV: So she lay at his feet until morning, but got up before one person could recognize another; for he said, “It must not be known that the woman came to the threshing floor.”
text.Rotherham: So she lay at his feet, until the morning, and rose up before one could know his neighbour. And he said—Do not let it be known that a woman came into the threshing-floor.
text.KJV: And she lay at his feet until the morning: and she rose up before one could know another. And he said, Let it not be known that a woman came into the floor.
text.NABRE: So she lay at his feet until morning, but rose before anyone could recognize another, for Boaz had said, “Let it not be known that this woman came to the threshing floor.”
text.DRB: She slept therefore at his feet till the night was gone. Therefore she arose before men could know one another, and Booz said: Beware lest any man know that thou camest hither.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Bring the cloak you are wearing and hold it out.” So she held it, and he measured out six measures of barley, and put it on her back; then he went into the city.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Bring the cloak that is upon thee, and hold it. So she held it,—and he measured six measures of barley, and laid it upon her, and he went into the city.
text.KJV: Also he said, Bring the vail that thou hast upon thee, and hold it. And when she held it, he measured six measures of barley, and laid it on her: and she went into the city.
text.NABRE: Then he said to her, “Take off the shawl you are wearing; hold it firmly.” When she did so, he poured out six measures of barley and helped her lift the bundle; then he himself left for the town.
text.DRB: And again, Spread, quoth he, thy mantle, wherewith thou art covered, and hold it with both hands. Who spreading and holding it, he measured six measures of barley, and put it upon her. Who carrying it entered into the city,

text.NRSV: She came to her mother-in-law, who said, “How did things go with you, my daughter?” Then she told her all that the man had done for her,
text.Rotherham: And, when she came unto her mother-in- law, she said—Who art, thou, my daughter? And she told her all that the man had done for her.
text.KJV: And when she came to her mother in law, she said, Who art thou, my daughter? And she told her all that the man had done to her.
text.NABRE: She, meanwhile, went home to her mother-in-law, who asked, “How did things go, my daughter?” So she told her all the man had done for her,
text.DRB: and came to her mother in law. Who said to her: What hast thou done daughter? And she told her all things, that the man had done to her.

text.NRSV: saying, “He gave me these six measures of barley, for he said, ‘Do not go back to your mother-in-law empty-handed.’”
text.Rotherham: And she said—These six measures of barley, gave he unto me,—for he said, Do not go in empty, unto thy mother-in- law.
text.KJV: And she said, These six measures of barley gave he me; for he said to me, Go not empty unto thy mother in law.
text.NABRE: and concluded, “He gave me these six measures of barley and said, ‘Do not go back to your mother-in-law empty.’”
text.DRB: And she said: Behold six measures of barley hath he given me, and he said: I will not have thee return empty to thy mother in law.

text.NRSV: She replied, “Wait, my daughter, until you learn how the matter turns out, for the man will not rest, but will settle the matter today.”
text.Rotherham: And she said—Abide, my daughter, until that thou get to know, how the matter will fall out,—for the man will not rest, except he have finished the thing to-day.
text.KJV: Then said she, Sit still, my daughter, until thou know how the matter will fall: for the man will not be in rest, until he have finished the thing this day.
text.NABRE: Naomi then said, “Wait here, my daughter, until you learn what happens, for the man will not rest, but will settle the matter today.”
text.DRB: And Noemi said: Expect daughter till we see what end the thing will have. For the man will not cease until he have accomplished that which he hath spoken.

text.NRSV: No sooner had Boaz gone up to the gate and sat down there than the next-of-kin, of whom Boaz had spoken, came passing by. So Boaz said, “Come over, friend; sit down here.” And he went over and sat down.
text.Rotherham: Now, Boaz, went up to the gate, and sat him down there, and lo! the kinsman, passing by, of whom Boaz had spoken, so he said—Turn aside! and sit down here, such a one! And he turned aside, and sat down.
text.KJV: Then went Boaz up to the gate, and sat him down there: and, behold, the kinsman of whom Boaz spake came by; unto whom he said, Ho, such a one! turn aside, sit down here. And he turned aside, and sat down.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Boaz Marries Ruth. Boaz went to the gate and took a seat there. Along came the other redeemer of whom he had spoken. Boaz called to him by name, “Come, sit here.” And he did so.
text.DRB: Booz therefore went up to the gate, and sat there. And when he had seen the nigh kinsman pass by, of whom the talk was had before, he said to him: Turn in a little while, and sit here: calling him by his name. Who turned in, and sat.

text.NRSV: Then Boaz took ten men of the elders of the town, and said, “Sit down here”; so they sat down.
text.Rotherham: Then fetched he ten men of the elders of the city, and said—Sit ye down here. And they sat down.
text.KJV: And he took ten men of the elders of the city, and said, Sit ye down here. And they sat down.
text.NABRE: Then Boaz picked out ten of the elders of the town and asked them to sit nearby. When they had done this,
text.DRB: And Booz taking ten men of the city, said to them: Sit ye here.

text.NRSV: He then said to the next-of-kin, “Naomi, who has come back from the country of Moab, is selling the parcel of land that belonged to our kinsman Elimelech.
text.Rotherham: Then said he to the kinsman, The parcel of land that was our brother Elimelech’s, is to be disposed of by Naomi, who hath returned out of the country of Moab;
text.KJV: And he said unto the kinsman, Naomi, that is come again out of the country of Moab, selleth a parcel of land, which was our brother Elimelech’s:
text.NABRE: he said to the other redeemer: “Naomi, who has come back from the plateau of Moab, is putting up for sale the piece of land that belonged to our kinsman Elimelech.
text.DRB: Who sitting down, he spake to the nigh kinsman: Noemi, who is returned from the country of Moab, will sell the part of the field belonging to our brother Elimelech.

text.NRSV: So I thought I would tell you of it, and say: Buy it in the presence of those sitting here, and in the presence of my people’s elders. If you will redeem it, redeem it; but if you will not, tell me, so that I may know; for there is no one prior to you to redeem it, and I come after you.” So he said, “I will redeem it.”
text.Rotherham: and, I, thought, I would unveil thine ear, saying—Take it over in presence of such as are here seated, and in presence of the elders of my people. If thou wilt act as kinsman, act as kinsman, but, if thou wilt not so act, only tell me—that I may know, for there is none who can set thee aside as kinsman, but, I, am after thee. And he said, I, will act as kinsman.
text.KJV: And I thought to advertise thee, saying, Buy it before the inhabitants, and before the elders of my people. If thou wilt redeem it, redeem it: but if thou wilt not redeem it, then tell me, that I may know: for there is none to redeem it beside thee; and I am after thee. And he said, I will redeem it.
text.NABRE: So I thought I would inform you. Before those here present, including the elders of my people, purchase the field; act as redeemer. But if you do not want to do it, tell me so, that I may know, for no one has a right of redemption prior to yours, and mine is next.” He answered, “I will act as redeemer.”
text.DRB: Which I would thee to understand, and would tell thee before all that sit, and the ancients of my people. If thou wilt possess it by the right of nigh kindred, buy, and possess it. But if it please thee not, tell me the same, that I may know what I ought to do. For there is no nigh kinsman saving thee, which art first, and me, who am second. But he answered: I will buy the field.

text.NRSV: Then Boaz said, “The day you acquire the field from the hand of Naomi, you are also acquiring Ruth the Moabite, the widow of the dead man, to maintain the dead man’s name on his inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Boaz, What day thou takest over the land from the hand of Naomi, also, of Ruth the Moabitess, wife of the dead, dost thou take [it], to raise up the name of the dead upon his inheritance.
text.KJV: Then said Boaz, What day thou buyest the field of the hand of Naomi, thou must buy it also of Ruth the Moabitess, the wife of the dead, to raise up the name of the dead upon his inheritance.
text.NABRE: Boaz continued, “When you acquire the field from Naomi, you also acquire responsibility for Ruth the Moabite, the widow of the late heir, to raise up a family for the deceased on his estate.”
text.DRB: To whom Booz said: When thou shalt buy the field at the woman's hand, thou must take also Ruth the Moabite, which was the wife of the deceased: that thou mayest raise up the name of thy kinsman in his inheritance.

text.NRSV: At this, the next-of-kin said, “I cannot redeem it for myself without damaging my own inheritance. Take my right of redemption yourself, for I cannot redeem it.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the kinsman—I cannot act as kinsman for myself, lest I mar my own inheritance,—do, thou, for thyself act as kinsman in my right, for I cannot so redeem.
text.KJV: And the kinsman said, I cannot redeem it for myself, lest I mar mine own inheritance: redeem thou my right to thyself; for I cannot redeem it.
text.NABRE: The redeemer replied, “I cannot exercise my right of redemption for that would endanger my own estate. You do it in my place, for I cannot.”
text.DRB: Who answered: I yield my right of nigh kindred: for I may not abolish the posterity of mine own family. Do thou use my privilege, which I profess that I do willingly forgo.

text.NRSV: Now this was the custom in former times in Israel concerning redeeming and exchanging: to confirm a transaction, one party took off a sandal and gave it to the other; this was the manner of attesting in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, this aforetime, [was the way] in Israel, at a redeeming, and at an exchanging, to confirm every word: A man drew off his shoe, and gave it to his neighbour,—yea, this, was the way of taking to witness, in Israel.
text.KJV: Now this was the manner in former time in Israel concerning redeeming and concerning changing, for to confirm all things; a man plucked off his shoe, and gave it to his neighbour: and this was a testimony in Israel.
text.NABRE: Now it used to be the custom in Israel that, to make binding a contract of redemption or exchange, one party would take off a sandal and give it to the other. This was the form of attestation in Israel.
text.DRB: And this in old time was the manner in Israel between kinsmen, that if at any time one yielded to another his right: that the grant might be sure, the man put off his shoe, & gave it to his neighbour, this was a testimony of yielding in Israel.

text.NRSV: So when the next-of-kin said to Boaz, “Acquire it for yourself,” he took off his sandal.
text.Rotherham: So the kinsman said unto Boaz, Take it over for thyself,—and he drew off his shoe.
text.KJV: Therefore the kinsman said unto Boaz, Buy it for thee. So he drew off his shoe.
text.NABRE: So the other redeemer, in saying to Boaz, “Acquire it for yourself,” drew off his sandal.
text.DRB: Booz therefore said to his kinsman: Take off thy shoe. Which immediately he loosed from his foot.

text.NRSV: Then Boaz said to the elders and all the people, “Today you are witnesses that I have acquired from the hand of Naomi all that belonged to Elimelech and all that belonged to Chilion and to Mahlon.
text.Rotherham: Then said Boaz to the elders, and all the people—Witnesses, are ye to-day, that I have taken over all that was Elimelech’s, and all that was Chilion’s, and Mahlon’s,—from the hand of Naomi:
text.KJV: And Boaz said unto the elders, and unto all the people, Ye are witnesses this day, that I have bought all that was Elimelech’s, and all that was Chilion’s and Mahlon’s, of the hand of Naomi.
text.NABRE: Boaz then said to the elders and to all the people, “You are witnesses today that I have acquired from Naomi all the holdings of Elimelech, Chilion and Mahlon.
text.DRB: But to the ancients, & the whole people he said: You are witnesses this day, that I have purchased all things which were Elimelech's, and Chelion's and Mahalon's, Noemi delivering them:

text.NRSV: Moreover I have acquired Ruth the Moabite, the wife of Mahlon, to be my wife, to maintain the name of the dead on his inheritance, in order that the name of the dead may not be cut off from his kindred and from the gate of his native place; today you are witnesses.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover, Ruth the Moabitess, wife of Mahlon, have I taken over, to be my wife, to raise up the name of the dead upon his inheritance, that the name of the dead be not cut off—from among his brethren, and from the gate of his dwelling- place,—Witnesses, are ye to-day!
text.KJV: Moreover Ruth the Moabitess, the wife of Mahlon, have I purchased to be my wife, to raise up the name of the dead upon his inheritance, that the name of the dead be not cut off from among his brethren, and from the gate of his place: ye are witnesses this day.
text.NABRE: I also acquire Ruth the Moabite, the widow of Mahlon, as my wife, in order to raise up a family for her late husband on his estate, so that the name of the deceased may not perish from his people and his place. Do you witness this today?”
text.DRB: & have taken in marriage Ruth the Moabite, the wife of Mahalon, that I may raise up the name of the deceased in his inheritance, lest his name be abolished out of his family and brethren and people. You, I say, are witnesses of this thing.

text.NRSV: Then all the people who were at the gate, along with the elders, said, “We are witnesses. May the Lord make the woman who is coming into your house like Rachel and Leah, who together built up the house of Israel. May you produce children in Ephrathah and bestow a name in Bethlehem;
text.Rotherham: Then said all the people who were in the gate, and the elders—Witnesses!,—Yahweh grant the woman who is coming into thy house, to be as Rachel, and as Leah, which two of them did build the house of Israel. Do thou bravely, then, in Ephrathah, and proclaim thou a name in Bethlehem,
text.KJV: And all the people that were in the gate, and the elders, said, We are witnesses. The Lord make the woman that is come into thine house like Rachel and like Leah, which two did build the house of Israel: and do thou worthily in Ephratah, and be famous in Beth–lehem:
text.NABRE: All those at the gate, including the elders, said, “We do. May the Lord make this woman come into your house like Rachel and Leah, who between them built up the house of Israel. Prosper in Ephrathah! Bestow a name in Bethlehem!
text.DRB: All the people that was in the gate answered, & the ancients: We are witnesses: Our Lord make this woman, which entereth into thy house, as Rachel, and Lia, which builded the house of Israel: that she may be an example of virtue in Ephrata, and may have a famous name in Bethlehem:

text.NRSV: and, through the children that the Lord will give you by this young woman, may your house be like the house of Perez, whom Tamar bore to Judah.”
text.Rotherham: And let thy house be like the house of Perez, whom Tamar bare to Judah,—of the seed which—may Yahweh give thee, of this young woman.
text.KJV: And let thy house be like the house of Pharez, whom Tamar bare unto Judah, of the seed which the Lord shall give thee of this young woman.
text.NABRE: With the offspring the Lord will give you from this young woman, may your house become like the house of Perez, whom Tamar bore to Judah.”
text.DRB: and that thy house may be, as the house of Phares, whom Thamar bare to Judas, of the seed which our Lord shall give thee of this young woman.

text.NRSV: So Boaz took Ruth and she became his wife. When they came together, the Lord made her conceive, and she bore a son.
text.Rotherham: So Boaz took Ruth, and she became his wife, and he went in unto her,—and Yahweh granted her conception, and she bare a son.
text.KJV: So Boaz took Ruth, and she was his wife: and when he went in unto her, the Lord gave her conception, and she bare a son.
text.NABRE: Boaz took Ruth. When they came together as husband and wife, the Lord enabled her to conceive and she bore a son.
text.DRB: Booz therefore took Ruth, and had her to wife: and went in unto her and our Lord gave her to conceive, and to bear a son.

text.NRSV: Then the women said to Naomi, “Blessed be the Lord, who has not left you this day without next-of-kin; and may his name be renowned in Israel!
text.Rotherham: Then said the women unto Naomi, Blessed, be Yahweh! who hath not let thee fail of a kinsman to-day,—and may his name, be proclaimed, in Israel;
text.KJV: And the women said unto Naomi, Blessed be the Lord, which hath not left thee this day without a kinsman, that his name may be famous in Israel.
text.NABRE: Then the women said to Naomi, “Blessed is the Lord who has not failed to provide you today with a redeemer. May he become famous in Israel!
text.DRB: And the women said to Noemi: Blessed be our Lord, which hath not suffered that there should fail a successor of thy family: That his name should be called in Israel.

text.NRSV: He shall be to you a restorer of life and a nourisher of your old age; for your daughter-in-law who loves you, who is more to you than seven sons, has borne him.”
text.Rotherham: So shall he become a restorer of thy life, and a nourisher of thine old age,—for, thy daughter-in-law who loveth thee, hath borne him, even, she, who is better to thee than seven sons.
text.KJV: And he shall be unto thee a restorer of thy life, and a nourisher of thine old age: for thy daughter in law, which loveth thee, which is better to thee than seven sons, hath born him.
text.NABRE: He will restore your life and be the support of your old age, for his mother is the daughter-in-law who loves you. She is worth more to you than seven sons!”
text.DRB: And thou shouldst have one that may comfort thy soul, and cherish thy old age. For of thy daughter in law is he born, which will love thee: and much better is she to thee, than if thou hadst seven sons.

text.NRSV: Then Naomi took the child and laid him in her bosom, and became his nurse.
text.Rotherham: So Naomi took the boy, and laid him in her bosom, and she became his nurse.
text.KJV: And Naomi took the child, and laid it in her bosom, and became nurse unto it.
text.NABRE: Naomi took the boy, cradled him against her breast, and cared for him.
text.DRB: And Noemi taking the child put it in her bosom, & did the office of a nurse & of one that should carry him.

text.NRSV: The women of the neighborhood gave him a name, saying, “A son has been born to Naomi.” They named him Obed; he became the father of Jesse, the father of David.
text.Rotherham: And the women, her neighbours, gave him a name, saying, There is born a son to Naomi,—So they called his name Obed, he, was, the father of Jesse, the father of David.
text.KJV: And the women her neighbours gave it a name, saying, There is a son born to Naomi; and they called his name Obed: he is the father of Jesse, the father of David.
text.NABRE: The neighbor women joined the celebration: “A son has been born to Naomi!” They named him Obed. He was the father of Jesse, the father of David.
text.DRB: And the women her neighbours congratulating her, & saying: There is a son born to Noemi: called his name Obed: this is the father of Isai, the father of David.

text.NRSV: Now these are the descendants of Perez: Perez became the father of Hezron,
text.Rotherham: These, then, are the generations of Perez: Perez, begat, Hezron;
text.KJV: Now these are the generations of Pharez: Pharez begat Hezron,
text.NABRE: These are the descendants of Perez: Perez was the father of Hezron,
text.DRB: These are the generations of Phares: Phares begat Esron,

text.NRSV: Hezron of Ram, Ram of Amminadab,
text.Rotherham: And, Hezron, begat, Ram, and, Ram, begat, Amminadab;
text.KJV: And Hezron begat Ram, and Ram begat Amminadab,
text.NABRE: Hezron was the father of Ram, Ram was the father of Amminadab,
text.DRB: Esron begat Aram: Aram begat Aminadab,

text.NRSV: Amminadab of Nahshon, Nahshon of Salmon,
text.Rotherham: And, Amminadab, begat Nahshon, and, Nahshon, begat, Salmon,
text.KJV: And Amminadab begat Nahshon, and Nahshon begat Salmon,
text.NABRE: Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, Nahshon was the father of Salma,
text.DRB: Aminadab begat Nahasson, Nahasson begat Salmon,

text.NRSV: Salmon of Boaz, Boaz of Obed,
text.Rotherham: And, Salmon, begat, Boaz, and, Boaz, begat, Obed;
text.KJV: And Salmon begat Boaz, and Boaz begat Obed,
text.NABRE: Salma was the father of Boaz, Boaz was the father of Obed,
text.DRB: Salmon begat Booz, Booz begat Obed,

text.NRSV: Obed of Jesse, and Jesse of David.
text.Rotherham: And, Obed, begat, Jesse, and, Jesse, begat, David.
text.KJV: And Obed begat Jesse, and Jesse begat David.
text.NABRE: Obed was the father of Jesse, and Jesse became the father of David.
text.DRB: Obed begat Isai, Isai begat David.
